action.reasoning:
Observation: Trained 35 epochs, train loss 1.36, val exprate stuck at 2-3%. The greedy decode test shows the model predicts plausible-looking ids. The checkpoint cfg has d_model=256, 3 decoder layers — only 6.4M params, very small. Loss = 1.36 with label_smoothing=0.1 actually corresponds to fairly poor token-level fit, but exprate=3% is bizarre. Let me actually check what the predictions look like vs ground truth to debug. Maybe there's a token decode issue. Let me run predict on val set and inspect.

Analysis: Token decode skips ids <= 3 (pad/sos/eos/unk). But what if the model is producing many `<unk>` (id=3) which we strip but ground truth has real tokens? Or maybe encoder mask issue causes the model to fail on padded test images. Let me directly inspect predictions vs ground truth.

Decision: First, run a debug script to look at actual val predictions vs targets to understand the gap.
